evals.name: active-rule-clarify
evals.prompt: A user asks ForgeFlow to clarify a medium task. The repository has an active project evolution rule: Rule ID artifact-first-no-chat-only, Scope project, Lifecycle active, Trigger when preparing ForgeFlow task artifacts or handoff notes, Application Stage clarify, Expected Behavior create or reference the active task directory and record Applied Evolution Rules instead of keeping evidence chat-only. Produce only the clarify-stage brief artifact content for task id eval-active-rule-clarify.
evals.expected_output: A brief artifact that includes Applied Evolution Rules, treats the project active rule as required, names the task directory, and avoids chat-only evidence.
assertions: Output includes an Applied Evolution Rules section.
Output references artifact-first-no-chat-only as a project active rule or required constraint.
Output identifies a concrete task artifact path under .forgeflow/tasks/.
Output does not treat the project active rule as merely advisory.

evals.name: propose-rule-long-run
evals.prompt: A long-run retrospective found the same mistake in three ForgeFlow tasks: final evidence was reported in chat but no ship-summary.md or implementation-notes.md was updated. Create the long-run evolution output that should propose a reusable rule. Produce the eval-record Evolution Rule Candidates section and one proposed evolution-rule.md candidate for this pattern. Keep it pure Markdown; do not mention Python runtime or JSON schema restoration.
evals.expected_output: An eval-record candidate plus proposed rule content with trigger, application stage, expected behavior, evidence, false-positive guard, rollback/retirement, and review status.
assertions: Output includes an Evolution Rule Candidates section.

Output includes all required evolution-rule template fields: Rule ID, Scope, Lifecycle, Trigger, Expected Behavior, Application Stage, Enforcement Mode, Evidence, False Positive Guard, Rollback / Retirement, Review Status.
Output uses proposed lifecycle and unreviewed review status.
Output avoids Python runtime or JSON schema restoration.

evals.name: review-rule-boundary
evals.prompt: Review this proposed ForgeFlow evolution rule: Scope global-advisory, Enforcement Mode required_project_rule, Trigger any failed test, Application Stage execute, Expected Behavior block every future task until the exact same command passes, Evidence vague: 'it happened before', False Positive Guard empty, Review Status unreviewed. Produce only the Evolution Rule Review section of review-report.md.
evals.expected_output: A review section that rejects or requests changes because global-advisory cannot hard block, evidence is insufficient, and the false-positive guard is missing.
assertions: Output marks the rule as changes_requested or rejected rather than approved.
Output identifies the invalid global-advisory hard enforcement boundary.
Output identifies missing or insufficient evidence.
Output identifies the missing false-positive guard.

evals.name: fan-out-execute-ledger
evals.prompt: You are executing a high-route ForgeFlow plan with fan-out/fan-in. Two independent plan steps ran in parallel via specialist subagents (Step A: src/api/auth.ts only; Step B: src/ui/login.tsx only). Both report DONE. Produce only the run-ledger.md fragment for both tasks after controller verification, plus one implementation-notes.md Evidence line each for micro_spec. Assume controller ran git diff --stat and step verification for both; spec micro-check PASS for both. Use Assignee discipline from skills/execute/SKILL.md.
evals.expected_output: Run-ledger entries with specialist assignees during work, done status with evidence refs, micro_spec:PASS per step, and no step marked done from self-report alone.
assertions: Output includes run-ledger task entries for both parallel steps.
Output uses Assignee values worker, specialist, or spec-reviewer appropriately.
Output records micro_spec:PASS or equivalent spec micro-gate evidence per step.
Output states that agent DONE self-report alone is insufficient for done status.

evals.name: review-micro-gates-table
evals.prompt: You are running /forgeflow:review --type spec on a completed high-route task. implementation-notes.md contains: micro_spec:PASS step=Add auth handler, micro_spec:PASS step=Add login UI. run-ledger shows both steps done with Assignee specialist. Produce only the Execute Micro-Gates section and Evidence Classification reported-evidence bullet for review-report.md. Mark stage re-verified as no until you independently inspect code.
evals.expected_output: Execute Micro-Gates table with both steps, micro_spec PASS, reported evidence listing micro_* lines, and explicit that micro-gates are not approval without re-verification.
assertions: Output includes an Execute Micro-Gates table or equivalent structured summary.
Output lists both plan steps with micro_spec PASS from execute.
Output treats micro-gate results as reported evidence, not observed approval.
Output indicates stage re-verified is no or pending independent verification.
